## Summary

This week focused on two threads: (1) tightening the main manuscript narrative around interpretable operator-level mechanisms, and (2) building an explicit exploratory analysis pipeline to explain the high non-normality observed in HF gait despite lower dimensionality and steeper normalized harmonic fall-off. 

## Accomplishments

- Main analysis updates:
  - Updated speed-matching so AB is capped at the HF maximum speed (127 cm/s), rather than LF maximum speed.
  - Revised manuscript wording/captions to align with those changes.
  - Added hierarchical bootstrap confidence intervals for all main effects in the manuscript.
  - Added several new exploratory analyses to probe mechanistic interpretations of the main findings, particularly around the HF non-normality pattern.
- New exploratory pipeline:
  - Added `Figure 5.jl` as a standalone exploratory script (not required for the core manuscript figures).
  - Script computes trial-level and subject-level mechanistic metrics, speed sensitivities, attenuation models, and clinical associations.
  - Clinical score merge implemented from subject-level Berg and Fugl-Meyer files.
- New exploratory figures generated:
  - `fig5a_resolvent_norm_curve.svg`: group median resolvent breadth curves, normalized by each trial's fundamental.
  - `fig5b_eta_speed_slope.svg`: subject-level speed sensitivity of dynamical strain.
  - `fig5c_hf_attenuation.svg`: incremental attenuation of HF effect as components are added.
  - `fig5d_berg_scatter.svg`: Berg vs rank intercept and rank slope.
  - `fig5f1_gain_per_rank.svg`: fundamental gain normalized by rank.

## Results and Figures

Key exploratory quantitative results from `Figure 5.jl`:

- Speed sensitivity (subject-level medians):
  - AB: `eta_slope ~ 0.0000`
  - HF: `eta_slope ~ 0.0000` (broader spread than AB)
  - LF: `eta_slope ~ 0.0061`
- HF attenuation on concentration outcome (`log10(C1)`):
  - group+speed: `beta_HF = 0.7335`
  - +rank: `beta_HF = 0.5142`
  - +eigdist: `beta_HF = 0.3053`
  - +eta: `beta_HF = 0.3117`
- Interpretation of attenuation pattern:
  - A large share of HF concentration is explained by rank and eigenvalue proximity.
  - Adding eta after those terms changes little for `C1`.
- Berg correlations (stroke subjects, n=13):
  - `rank_int`: `r = 0.754` (95% CI [0.219, 0.947])
  - `rank_slope`: `r = -0.810` (95% CI [-0.938, -0.484])

These findings support a tradeoff framing: AB shows broader, distributed responsiveness; HF appears more concentrated (high fundamental emphasis relative to available dimensions); LF appears lower-capacity overall.


## Challenges / Open Questions

- Visual interpretability remains the main challenge:
  - Some exploratory panels were initially too abstract/noisy and required simplification.
  - Need to decide which exploratory figures are explanatory enough for the manuscript versus supplement.
- Sample size for clinical linkage is still small (`n=13` with complete Berg/Fugl), so uncertainty remains wide for many effects.
- Need to avoid over-interpretation of metrics (e.g., gain/rank) as causal measures without stronger mechanistic justification.

## Plan for Next Week

- Tighten narrative around operator-level mechanisms, their implications, and tradeoffs in main text. Some figures will be moved to supplement if they are not needed for the core narrative.
- Finalize placement and wording of exploratory panels now integrated into `index.qmd`:
  - Keep B1, C, D1/D2 (Berg), and F1 as currently selected.
- Tighten text around the concentration-vs-breadth interpretation. Determine whether further simplification or clearer analyses are needed to make the key points more understandable.
- Decide whether to keep exploratory content in main text or move part to supplement after feedback.
- Will probably primarily work on the perspective paper while waiting for feedback on the main manuscript.
